title: Bubble Sort Implementation
objective: To understand and implement the Bubble Sort algorithm in Python, identifying its time complexity and potential optimizations.
steps: 1. Define a list of unsorted numbers (e.g., [64, 34, 25, 12, 22, 11, 90]).
2. Write an outer loop that iterates over the entire array length 'n'.
3. Write an inner loop that iterates from 0 to 'n - i - 1'.
4. Inside the inner loop, compare adjacent elements. If the left element is greater than the right, swap them.
5. Add a boolean flag to track if any swaps occurred in the inner loop. If no swaps occurred, break the outer loop (optimization).

rubric.q1: What is the worst-case time complexity of Bubble Sort and why?
rubric.q2: How does the boolean flag optimization improve the best-case time complexity?
rubric.q3: Why does the inner loop only run up to 'n - i - 1' instead of 'n'?
